data.content:
The file is generated successfully (`obj/Debug/net8.0/Generated/GeneratedInfo.cs`) but the compiler doesn't see it. The problem: **SDK-style projects expand file globs at evaluation time**, before any targets run. Since the file doesn't exist yet during evaluation, it's never added to the `Compile` items.

**Fix:** Add the generated file to the `Compile` and `FileWrites` item groups inside the target.

arguments.new_str:
<Message Importance="high" Text="Generated code file: $(_GeneratedFilePath)" />

    <!-- Add the generated file to compilation and track for Clean -->
    <ItemGroup>
      <Compile Include="$(_GeneratedFilePath)" />
      <FileWrites Include="$(_GeneratedFilePath)" />
    </ItemGroup>
  </Target>

data.reasoningText:
The issue is clear. The `GenerateSampleCode` target runs `BeforeTargets="CoreCompile;BeforeCompile"` and generates the file, but it never adds the generated file to the `Compile` item group. Since SDK-style projects expand globs at evaluation time (before any targets run), the generated file isn't picked up by the implicit glob.

The fix is to add the generated file to the `<Compile>` item group within the target, after the file is written. I should also add it to `<FileWrites>` so it gets cleaned properly.

Let me fix this.

arguments.new_str:
<Message Importance="high" Text="Generated code file: $(_GeneratedFilePath)" />

    <!-- Add the generated file to compilation and track for Clean -->
    <ItemGroup>
      <Compile Include="$(_GeneratedFilePath)" />
      <FileWrites Include="$(_GeneratedFilePath)" />
    </ItemGroup>
  </Target>

data.content:
**Root cause:** SDK-style projects expand implicit `Compile` globs at **evaluation time** — before any targets execute. The generated file didn't exist yet, so it was never included in compilation.

**Fix:** Added an `<ItemGroup>` inside the `GenerateSampleCode` target to explicitly add the generated file to:
- `<Compile>` — so the compiler picks it up
- `<FileWrites>` — so `dotnet clean` removes it
